title: Fix booking submissions end-to-end: backend persistence, query hook, and admin display

requirements.text: Audit and rewrite the Motoko backend `submitBooking` function in `backend/main.mo` to guarantee reliable persistence of every submission. Ensure the stable `submissions` Map (or equivalent stable structure) is correctly declared with `stable` keyword, properly initialized, and that `submitBooking` always appends the new entry with an auto-incremented ID and returns `#ok`. Add a `getAllSubmissions` public query function that returns all stored submissions as an array of records including id, name, email, phone, eventType, eventDate, venue, guestCount, additionalDetails, and submittedAt timestamp. Eliminate any pattern that could cause silent failures or state loss across canister upgrades.

quotes: Bookings still do not show up

acceptanceCriteria: Submitting a booking form always results in the entry being stored and retrievable via getAllSubmissions
The stable map/array persists across upgrades without data loss
getAllSubmissions returns all previously stored entries in an array
submitBooking always returns #ok on valid input and never silently drops data
Each submission record includes: id, name, email, phone, eventType, eventDate, venue, guestCount, additionalDetails, submittedAt

requirements.text: Rewrite the `useGetAllSubmissions` hook in `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` to call the correct backend query method (`getAllSubmissions`) using an anonymous actor (no authentication required). Ensure the hook correctly unpacks the returned array, handles both empty arrays and populated arrays, and does not depend on Internet Identity being logged in. The query should run automatically on page load with no manual trigger.

quotes: Bookings still do not show up

acceptanceCriteria: useGetAllSubmissions calls actor.getAllSubmissions() using an anonymous actor
The hook runs automatically on mount without requiring login
Returned data is correctly typed and mapped to the submission record shape
Empty array is handled gracefully (shows empty state, no crash)
Populated array is surfaced to the component as an array of submission objects

requirements.text: Update `frontend/src/pages/AdminPage.tsx` to consume the fixed `useGetAllSubmissions` hook and render all returned submission records as styled cards. Each card must display: name, email, phone, event type, event date, venue, guest count, additional details, and submission timestamp. Remove any remaining authentication check or login gate. Show a clear empty-state message when no submissions exist. Show a loading indicator while the query is in flight. Show an error message if the query fails.

quotes: Bookings still do not show up

acceptanceCriteria: Admin page at /admin loads immediately without any login prompt or auth gate
All stored booking submissions are displayed as cards with all 9 fields visible
A loading spinner or skeleton is shown while submissions are being fetched
An error message is displayed if the fetch fails
An empty-state message is shown when there are no submissions
Page styling matches the site's black and neon green palette

requirements.text: Ensure `frontend/src/components/BookingForm.tsx` uses the `useSubmitForm` mutation hook correctly and displays clear success and error feedback after submission. The mutation must call the backend `submitBooking` function with all 8 fields (name, email, phone, eventType, eventDate, venue, guestCount, additionalDetails) using an anonymous actor. On success, show a confirmation message and reset the form. On error, show an error message without losing the entered data.

quotes: Bookings still do not show up

acceptanceCriteria: Submitting the booking form calls submitBooking on the backend with all 8 fields
A success confirmation message is displayed after a successful submission
The form resets after a successful submission
An error message is displayed if the submission fails, preserving form data
The mutation uses an anonymous actor so no login is required to submit

constraints: All Motoko logic must remain in the single actor in backend/main.mo
Do not require Internet Identity authentication for booking form submission or admin page viewing
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts and frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts are read-only and must not be modified
frontend/src/main.tsx and frontend/src/components/ui are read-only and must not be modified
nonGoals: Adding authentication or access control to the admin page
Changing the visual design of sections other than the admin page
Adding pagination or search/filter to the admin submissions list
